title: Dianthera collina
taxonomy: Dianthera collina is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. This plant is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world, including Central and South America and the Caribbean. Dianthera collina is an herbaceous plant that grows in wetland areas and is often found in freshwater environments. It is a member of the tribe Justicieae and is closely related to other plants in the same family. Dianthera collina is also known as the "Blue Water Speedwell" due to its blue flowers.

The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as reducing oxidative stress and exhibiting antimicrobial properties.
